key_insight: A physics-informed SVC trained on sparse experimental data identifies composition rules that can improve room-temperature ductility in tungsten-based refractory high-entropy alloys.
engineering_relevance: The model suggests practical alloying directions for fusion-relevant W-based RHEAs, especially moderate Ti, Ni, and Co additions and avoidance of high Cr contents.
method: Curated experimental dataset of tungsten-containing alloys with ductile/brittle labels
Built composition-based, physics-informed descriptors and compared three classifiers with nested cross-validation
Used SHAP and synthetic compositions on pseudo-ternary maps to interpret and visualize ductility trends
evidence_signal: The support vector classifier achieved the best generalization under nested cross-validation, and its predictions aligned with published experimental trends and DFT-derived elastic descriptors.
watch_item: The dataset is sparse and heterogeneous, so predictions should be treated as screening guidance rather than definitive alloy design rules.
paper_title: Ductility Design Rules for Tungsten based Refractory High Entropy Alloys from Sparse Experimental Datasets
